key_insight: Physics-Informed Broad Learning System (PIBLS) solves PDEs by turning the physics constraints into a direct least-squares problem, enabling fast, backpropagation-free training with a proven universal approximation guarantee.
engineering_relevance: PIBLS offers a practical mesh-free alternative for real-time simulation and design optimization when traditional solvers are too costly and PINNs are too slow or unstable.
method: Reformulate PDE solving as a least-squares optimization problem.
Use a broad learning system instead of backpropagation-based training.
Extend the framework to nonlinear PDEs and prove universal approximation.
evidence_signal: On linear and nonlinear PDE benchmarks, PIBLS is reported to be 1–3 orders of magnitude faster than conventional PINNs while achieving higher solution accuracy.
watch_item: The direct-solve approach may still depend on stable linear-system conditioning and careful formulation for complex nonlinear PDEs.
paper_title: Learning universal approximations for partial differential equations with Physics-Informed Broad Learning System
